quotes.quote_text: “ Your list of the common extent of accomplishments , ”

context.quote_text: “ All young ladies accomplished ! My dear Charles , what do you mean ? ”

“ Yes , all of them , I think . They all paint tables , cover screens , and net purses . I scarcely know any one who can not do all this ; and I am sure I never heard a young lady spoken of for the first time , without being informed that she was very accomplished . ”

“ has too much truth . The word is applied to many a woman who deserves it no otherwise than by netting a purse or covering a screen ; but I am very far from agreeing with you in your estimation of ladies in general . I can not boast of knowing more than half - a - dozen in the whole range of my acquaintance that are really accomplished . ”
“ Nor I , I am sure , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ has too much truth . The word is applied to many a woman who deserves it no otherwise than by netting a purse or covering a screen ; but I am very far from agreeing with you in your estimation of ladies in general . I can not boast of knowing more than half - a - dozen in the whole range of my acquaintance that are really accomplished . ”

context.quote_text: “ Yes , all of them , I think . They all paint tables , cover screens , and net purses . I scarcely know any one who can not do all this ; and I am sure I never heard a young lady spoken of for the first time , without being informed that she was very accomplished . ”
“ Your list of the common extent of accomplishments , ”
“ Nor I , I am sure , ”
“ Then , ”
“ Oh , certainly , ”

“ no one can be really esteemed accomplished who does not greatly surpass what is usually met with . A woman must have a thorough knowledge of music , singing , drawing , dancing , and the modern languages , to deserve the word ; and , besides all this , she must possess a certain something in her air and manner of walking , the tone of her voice , her address and expressions , or the word will be but half deserved . ”
“ and to all she must yet add something more substantial in the improvement of her mind by extensive reading . ”
“ I am no longer surprised at your knowing _ only _ six accomplished women . I rather wonder now at your knowing _ any _ . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ and to all she must yet add something more substantial in the improvement of her mind by extensive reading . ”

context.quote_text: “ no one can be really esteemed accomplished who does not greatly surpass what is usually met with . A woman must have a thorough knowledge of music , singing , drawing , dancing , and the modern languages , to deserve the word ; and , besides all this , she must possess a certain something in her air and manner of walking , the tone of her voice , her address and expressions , or the word will be but half deserved . ”
“ All this she must possess , ”
“ I am no longer surprised at your knowing _ only _ six accomplished women . I rather wonder now at your knowing _ any _ . ”
“ Are you so severe upon your own sex as to doubt the possibility of all this ? ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Are you so severe upon your own sex as to doubt the possibility of all this ? ”

context.quote_text: “ and to all she must yet add something more substantial in the improvement of her mind by extensive reading . ”
“ I am no longer surprised at your knowing _ only _ six accomplished women . I rather wonder now at your knowing _ any _ . ”
“ _ I _ never saw such a woman . _ I _ never saw such capacity , and taste , and application , and elegance , as you describe , united . ”
“ Eliza Bennet , ”
“ Eliza Bennet , ”

“ is one of those young ladies who seek to recommend themselves to the other sex by undervaluing their own ; and with many men , I daresay , it succeeds ; but , in my opinion , it is a paltry device , a very mean art . ”
“ there is meanness in _ all _ the arts which ladies sometimes condescend to employ for captivation . Whatever bears affinity to cunning is despicable . ”
“ Indeed I have , sir , ”

context.quote_text: “ is one of those young ladies who seek to recommend themselves to the other sex by undervaluing their own ; and with many men , I daresay , it succeeds ; but , in my opinion , it is a paltry device , a very mean art . ”
“ Undoubtedly , ”
“ Indeed I have , sir , ”
“ She is a great deal too ill to be moved . Mr. Jones says we must not think of moving her . We must trespass a little longer on your kindness . ”
“ that you were a studier of character . It must be an amusing study . ”
“ Yes ; but intricate characters are the _ most _ amusing . They have at least that advantage . ”
“ can in general supply but few subjects for such a study . In a country neighbourhood you move in a very confined and unvarying society . ”
“ But people themselves alter so much , that there is something new to be observed in them for ever . ”

context.quote_text: “ Yes ; but intricate characters are the _ most _ amusing . They have at least that advantage . ”
“ The country , ”
“ But people themselves alter so much , that there is something new to be observed in them for ever . ”
“ Yes , indeed , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Ay , that is because you have the right disposition . But that gentleman , ”

context.quote_text: “ When I am in the country , ”
“ I never wish to leave it ; and when I am in town , it is pretty much the same . They have each their advantages , and I can be equally happy in either . ”
“ seemed to think the country was nothing at all . ”
“ Indeed , mamma , you are mistaken , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ seemed to think the country was nothing at all . ”

context.quote_text: “ I never wish to leave it ; and when I am in town , it is pretty much the same . They have each their advantages , and I can be equally happy in either . ”
“ Ay , that is because you have the right disposition . But that gentleman , ”
“ Indeed , mamma , you are mistaken , ”
“ You quite mistook Mr. Darcy . He only meant that there was not such a variety of people to be met with in the country as in town , which you must acknowledge to be true . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Oh dear , yes ; but you must own she is very plain . Lady Lucas herself has often said so , and envied me Jane ’s beauty . I do not like to boast of my own child ; but to be sure , Jane -- one does not often see anybody better looking . It is what everybody says . I do not trust my own partiality . When she was only fifteen there was a gentleman at my brother Gardiner ’s in town so much in love with her , that my sister - in - law was sure he would make her an offer before we came away . But , however , he did not . Perhaps he thought her too young . However , he wrote some verses on her , and very pretty they were . ”

context.quote_text: “ No , she would go home . I fancy she was wanted about the mince - pies . For my part , Mr. Bingley , _ I _ always keep servants that can do their own work ; _ my _ daughters are brought up differently . But everybody is to judge for themselves , and the Lucases are a very good sort of girls , I assure you . It is a pity they are not handsome ! Not that _ I _ think Charlotte so _ very _ plain ; but then she is our particular friend . ”
“ She seems a very pleasant young woman , ”
“ And so ended his affection , ”
“ There has been many a one , I fancy , overcome in the same way . I wonder who first discovered the efficacy of poetry in driving away love ! ”

quotes.quote_text: “ I have been used to consider poetry as the _ food _ of love , ”

context.quote_text: “ And so ended his affection , ”
“ There has been many a one , I fancy , overcome in the same way . I wonder who first discovered the efficacy of poetry in driving away love ! ”

“ Of a fine , stout , healthy love it may . Everything nourishes what is strong already . But if it be only a slight , thin sort of inclination , I am convinced that one good sonnet will starve it entirely away . ”

“ I am perfectly ready , I assure you , to keep my engagement ; and , when your sister is recovered , you shall , if you please , name the very day of the ball . But you would not wish to be dancing while she is ill ? ”

quotes.quote_text: “ And this is always the way with him , ”

context.quote_text: “ That is not very likely ; our authority was too good . ”
“ He is certainly a good brother , ”
“ Whatever can give his sister any pleasure , is sure to be done in a moment . There is nothing he would not do for her . ”
“ delightful , ”
